body.title: First-class intentions store + chatlog backfill (recover deliberately-stated intentions)
body.text:
# D-intentions-store-2026-06-30
**title:** First-class intentions store + chatlog backfill
**date:** 2026-06-30
**status:** open
**spawned_tickets:** T-intentions-store, T-extract-intentions-from-chatlogs, T-consequence-D-intentions-store

## Decision narrative
Akien has asked repeatedly for an intentions folder; none exists. Intentions live embedded in decisions/ and architecture/, so the 'extant intention list' that /sorted must map against is only a grep. Create devlab/runtime/memory/intentions/ as a canonical store (memory_emit category + intention envelope + context-load surfacing), then backfill it by scanning chat history for the word 'intent' (used deliberately, with intent, for weeks) and classifying keepers via Haiku. Source verified: history is the ~375 harness JSONL transcripts, not the empty memory/chat.cc.0 or claude_chat_logs/. Keepers get an Akien-review checkpoint; conflicts trigger a joint Akien+CC reconciliation pass (intentions compete/merge/decompose).

## Hypothesis
After this ships, intentions are a grep-able first-class store; /sorted maps each new intention against real records; and the intentions Akien stated across weeks of chat are recovered rather than lost to scrollback.

## Measurement Signal
devlab/runtime/memory/intentions/ exists with schema-valid I-<slug> records; memory_emit accepts --category intentions; context-load prints an active-intentions line; the backfill logs candidate-count -> keeper-count; no NEW 'I intend that' statements get written embedded in decisions/architecture after the store exists.

## Intention
I intend that intentions are first-class living entities in their own store, situated against each other, and that Akien's deliberately-expressed intentions are recovered into it.
